meta.name: CRLF Injection
meta.description: CRLF (Carriage Return Line Feed) injection payloads for HTTP header injection, response splitting, cache poisoning, and log injection. \r\n shown as literal sequences.

payloads.description: URL-encoded CRLF header injection — %0d=CR, %0a=LF; injects custom header
LF-only injection — some servers accept LF without CR for header splitting
Cookie injection via CRLF — injects arbitrary cookie into response
XFF header injection via CRLF — may bypass IP-based access controls
CRLF to XSS — injects content-type and body for XSS via response splitting
HTTP response splitting — injects full fake HTTP response to poison next response
Advanced response splitting — sets Content-Length: 0 to terminate first response, starts new one
CRLF-based redirect injection — injects Location header for open redirect
Cache-Control injection — disable caching via CRLF to affect CDN/proxy behavior
Cache key poisoning via CRLF — injects invalid cache key to disrupt caching
CORS header injection via CRLF — injects permissive CORS headers into response
Log injection payload — injects fake access log entry to obscure attack traces
URL-encoded newline log injection — single LF to inject into log files
Unicode U+560A/U+560D CRLF bypass — some frameworks normalize these Unicode chars to CRLF
Fragment + CRLF combination — # truncates URL in client but server sees injected header
Literal backslash-r-n — some frameworks interpret \r\n as CRLF in header values
